title: Land the IO-6 hard-gate test behind a build tag so go test ./... stays green
description:
Split from bf-5312 (IO-6 hard-gate scenario + triage gate). Child 1 of 4 — the unblocker. Depends on nothing.

WHY bf-5312 FAILED 3x: NEEDLE runs `cd mothership && go test ./...` (no -short) on every bead close (.needle.yaml:42,67). The IO-6 hard-gate test TestIO6HardGate_WalkerProducesTrackedBlob currently lives in mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go with NO build tag, so the default suite picks it up. It is correctly strict and therefore RED (zero tracked blobs) until upstream bf-4q5w wires fusion Engine.SetNodePosition — so every close attempt fails NEEDLE's mandatory test run. The ~217 IO-6 lines are also currently UNCOMMITTED in the working tree.

SCOPE (do NOT weaken the assertion — it stays byte-for-byte strict):
- Move the IO-6-only code out of e2e_test.go into a NEW file mothership/tests/e2e/io6_gate_test.go with a `//go:build io6_gate` build constraint as the FIRST line. IO-6-only symbols (confirmed used nowhere else in the e2e dir): TestIO6HardGate_WalkerProducesTrackedBlob, CaptureIO6Diagnostics, io6RunMaxima, GetNodeRecords, GetStatus, StatusResponse.
- Leave the SHARED helpers (GetBlobCount, GetEvents, WatchDashboardWS, AssertBlobObserved, AssertDetectionEventsObserved, AssertDuringRun, RunSimulator) in e2e_test.go / assertions_test.go — UNTAGGED — because TestFullE2EIntegration and TestDetectionEvents reuse them.
- The assertion body is unchanged: still t.Fatalf on zero blobs, still NO skip-on-zero-blobs, still NO log-downgrade.
- Commit the move.
- Document the gated invocation as a runbook comment at the top of io6_gate_test.go: `cd mothership && go test -tags io6_gate -run TestIO6HardGate ./tests/e2e/...`

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `cd mothership && go test ./...` is GREEN (IO-6 gate excluded by the build tag — this is what unblocks NEEDLE closure for the sibling e2e work).
- `cd mothership && go vet ./...` is clean.
- `cd mothership && go test -tags io6_gate ./tests/e2e/...` COMPILES (it is expected to FAIL/RED until bf-4q5w; child 2 verifies the RED state).
- The assertion in TestIO6HardGate_WalkerProducesTrackedBlob is unchanged (strict).
- The change is committed.

title: Spatial quick actions (Component 32)
description:
## Goal
Right-click (desktop) or long-press (mobile) anywhere in 3D view to get context-sensitive actions based on what's under cursor.

## Scope
Actions per target:
- On blob: 'Who is this?' (BLE assignment), 'Why is this here?' (explainability), 'Follow' (camera tracks), 'Create automation here', 'Mark incorrect' (thumbs-down), 'Track history'
- On node: 'Diagnostics' (CSI plot), 'Blink LED', 'Reposition', 'Update firmware', 'Show links', 'Disable/Enable'
- On empty floor space: 'What happened here?' (filter timeline), 'Add trigger zone', 'Add virtual node', 'Coverage quality'
- On zone label: 'Zone history', 'Edit zone', 'Create automation', 'Crowd flow'
- On portal: 'Crossing log', 'Edit portal', 'Reverse direction'
- On trigger volume: 'Edit trigger', 'Test', 'View log', 'Disable/Enable'

## Implementation
Three.js Raycaster determines what's under cursor
Single context menu component renders appropriate options
Each action dispatches to existing dashboard functions (no new backend endpoints needed)

## Acceptance
- Right-click/long-press on blob shows blob-specific actions
- Right-click/long-press on node shows node-specific actions
- Right-click/long-press on empty space shows space-specific actions
- Actions execute correctly
- Works on both desktop (right-click) and mobile (long-press)

title: Build spaxel:0.1.354, deploy to ardenone-cluster, re-flash ESP32
description:
After bf-49fp (Dockerfile cache-bust fix) is merged:

1. Bump VERSION file to 0.1.354
2. Push to git to trigger spaxel-build CI on iad-ci
3. Monitor workflow until ronaldraygun/spaxel:0.1.354 is pushed
4. In ~/declarative-config, update the spaxel Deployment image tag to 0.1.354
5. Push declarative-config, ArgoCD syncs (Recreate strategy, ~30s downtime expected)
6. Verify mothership is healthy at spaxel.ardenone.com/healthz

Then re-flash the ESP32:
- Navigate to spaxel.ardenone.com provision page
- Connect ESP32-S3 via USB
- Run flash — mothership will now serve the 4MB firmware (no more crash loop)

Success criteria: ESP32 boots, starts captive portal or connects to WiFi, no reboot loop.
CI submit cmd: kubectl --kubeconfig=~/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig create -f - (spaxel-build workflow)

(2) PREREQUISITE SATISFIED: bf-49fp is CLOSED, merged as commit 301a588 (cache-bust / firmware crash-loop fix) and already deployed in 0.1.358. title: Capture end-to-end runtime logs and confirm zero undefined-field errors
description:
## Objective
Decomposed from parent bf-f841 (final step). Run the full identity-blob scenario end-to-end and produce a captured log artifact proving there are NO runtime errors from undefined/missing identity fields — the explicit goal of the parent's last acceptance criterion.

## Scope
- Run the complete runtime scenario established by the prior steps (blob created + identity populated + projections fed).
- Capture the runtime log / console output to an artifact (file or quoted log block).
- Grep/scan the captured log for panic, nil-pointer dereference, or any identity/undefined-field error.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full scenario runs to completion without the process crashing.
- [ ] A captured runtime log artifact is referenced in the bead.
- [ ] Scan of the log finds zero panic / nil-deref / undefined-field errors related to identity fields.

title: Strengthen TestDetectionEvents to assert at least one detection event
description:

CONTEXT: mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go:679 TestDetectionEvents currently only checks events != nil. The comment says "The endpoint must return a valid (possibly empty) events list" and t.Logf("Events API functional: found %d detection events", ...) — it explicitly ducks the count. This is one of the dodges called out in bf-5jeo.

SCOPE:
- Replace the nil-only check with a hard assertion that len(events.Events) >= 1, using the helper added in bf-2330.
- Provide an actionable failure message (include count observed + that the fusion pipeline produced no blobs).
- Keep the existing 4-node / 2-walker / 15s RunSimulator config and the timeout handling.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- TestDetectionEvents asserts >= 1 detection event (no longer accepts an empty list as pass).
- Failure message names the likely upstream cause (fusion/tracking produced no blobs).
- Test compiles (go build ./tests/e2e/...).
- NOTE: this test is expected to be RED until upstream bf-4q5w (fusion SetNodePosition wiring) lands — that strictness is the entire point of this capstone. Do NOT weaken the assertion to make it green.

title: Seed realistic node geometry in spaxel-sim default placement
description:
Split from bf-4q5w (parent umbrella). Depends on the runtime-propagation child.

Even with positions wired, the spaxel-sim default virtual-node placement leaves nodes co-located / at origin, so the Fresnel excess path length |P-T|+|P-R|-|T-R| collapses toward 0 everywhere and the accumulation grid has no meaningful peaks, producing no blobs (the core symptom in bf-4q5w).

Scope:
- In the simulator virtual-node creation path (createVirtualNodes and the simulator package, e.g. internal/simulator/registry_bridge.go where PosX/PosY/PosZ are sourced from node.Position), give the default virtual nodes a realistic spread-out geometry across a default zone instead of co-located/origin positions.
- Ensure these positions flow into both the registry and the fusion engine via the wiring from prior children.
- Pick a sane default room/zone size consistent with roomWidth/roomDepth used in main.go for the localization system.

Acceptance criteria:
- Default sim nodes occupy a spread, non-degenerate geometry (not all at origin, not co-located).
- A sim or host test shows the fusion accumulation grid produces non-zero peaks with the default placement.
- Existing simulator tests still pass; go build ./... passes.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Closed as complete — verified the full default-placement chain.

bf-18yn is the umbrella; its scope was implemented by the closed children bf-xrej (impl) and bf-1kid (acceptance test), both already on origin/main.

Verification (all green):
- go build ./... clean across all 3 modules (mothership, cmd/sim, test/acceptance)
- go test ./... — 50 packages pass
- go vet ./... clean across all 3 modules
- Acceptance test TestEngine_DefaultPlacementProducesPeaks passes for default placements of 2/3/4/6 nodes (uses ONLY simulator.DefaultNodePositions, no manual positioning)
- Counter-example TestEngine_CoLocatedOriginYieldsNoPeaks confirms the assertion is non-trivial (co-located (0,0,1) nodes correctly yield 0 blobs)

Scope coverage:
- Spread geometry: simulator.DefaultNodePositions (node.go) — single node at center, 2 on diagonal, 3+ on expanding grid with mixed Z. registry_bridge.effectivePositions reassigns any default-(0,0,1) origin node a distinct spread slot at sync time (SyncToRegistry/SyncOneNode/ToRegistryRecords).
- Registry flow: effectivePositions -> SyncToRegistry writes spread positions; bf-24xp persists hello-announced position.
- Fusion flow: main.go startup seeding loop (main.go:1054-1062) reads registry nodes and calls fusionEngine.SetNodePosition per node; bf-1tsm startup assertion guards distinct/non-(0,0,1) invariant.
- Room size: DefaultSpace is 6x5x2.5 (plan.md canonical sim example); DefaultNodePositions is parameterized by space.Bounds() so it works for any room incl. main.go's default 10x10x2.5.

No new code committed — work was already complete and pushed by the children. Nothing left to implement.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Closed out: spread node geometry for spaxel-sim default placement is implemented, committed, and verified against all three acceptance criteria.

Implementation (already committed by prior children of bf-4q5w, verified here):
- internal/simulator/node.go DefaultNodePositions(space,count) (bf-3fr6): distinct, in-bounds, room-spanning points — center for 1, opposite corners for 2, an expanding ceil(sqrt(n)) grid (>=2 cols & rows, alternating low/high Z band) for >=3. No two nodes co-locate and the geometry is never all-at-origin.
- internal/simulator/registry_bridge.go effectivePositions (bf-xrej): default-origin (0,0,1) virtual nodes are reassigned deterministic spread slots (sorted-ID order, sized to full node count, collision-free) at sync time so the registry and the fusion engine never see co-located nodes. SyncToRegistry/SyncOneNode/ToRegistryRecords all resolve through it; explicitly-placed nodes are preserved.
- cmd/sim generateNodePositions: the spaxel-sim CLI binary likewise seeds corners/grid, never origin.
- Default space 6x5x2.5 (sane residential default; bridge prefers the store's configured room, else DefaultSpace).

Acceptance criteria verified:
1. Spread non-degenerate geometry — DefaultNodePositions spans both X and Y for count>=2, all positions distinct. node_positions_test.go + registry_bridge_test.go pass.
2. Host test shows non-zero fusion peaks with default placement — TestEngine_DefaultPlacementProducesPeaks (the bf-18yn acceptance test, nodes=2/3/4/6) passes; TestEngine_GeometryPlacementDrivesFusionPeaks (bf-4b1c differential) shows spread->gridMax=1.0+blobs vs co-located(0,0,1)->gridMax=0.0+no blobs.
3. go build ./... passes across all three modules (mothership, cmd/sim, test/acceptance); go vet ./... clean; full mothership go test ./... green. Positions flow simulator->registry->fusion via the runtime wiring from bf-u7ds/bf-95tx/bf-3y9r.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Closed bf-18yn: default virtual nodes now receive spread, non-degenerate geometry instead of co-located/origin positions.

Three creation paths covered:
- cmd/sim createVirtualNodes -> generateNodePositions spreads nodes across corners/grid of the --space room (default 6x5x2.5).
- simulator.DefaultNodePositions (node.go): room-spanning grid placement, distinct cells, mixed-height Z.
- registry_bridge.effectivePositions: nodes still at the DB-default origin (0,0,1) are reassigned deterministic spread slots (DefaultNodePositions sized to the full node set) so the registry and downstream fusion engine never see co-located virtual nodes.
- api AddNode handler (commit 669d379): assigns spread positions for origin nodes, preserving explicitly-set positions.

Positions flow into the registry (SyncToRegistry/SyncOneNode) and into the fusion engine via the prior runtime-propagation child (bf-u7ds) plus main.go's startup SetNodePosition seeding loop.

Acceptance verified:
- TestEngine_DefaultPlacementProducesPeaks (fusion_test.go) is the explicit bf-18yn acceptance test: seeds nodes via DEFAULT placement only, drives a walker, asserts non-zero peaks (blobs>0 or gridMax>threshold) for node counts 2/3/4/6. Counter-example TestEngine_CoLocatedOriginYieldsNoPeaks confirms co-located nodes yield no peaks, so the assertion is non-trivial.
- go build ./... passes for mothership, cmd/sim, test/acceptance.
- go vet ./... clean; go test ./... green across all modules (incl. simulator, fusion, api, tests/e2e).

title: File the IO-6 RED-state finding with trace evidence back to bf-4q5w
description:

title: TX slot collision detection and adaptive re-stagger
description: Plan (Component 5 / Fleet Manager) specifies collision monitoring: if CSI frames from two TX nodes arrive within 3ms of each other, log a 'possible slot collision' metric. If collision rate > 5% over a 60-second window, re-randomize stagger assignments (shift one node's slot by half a slot width) and push updated config messages. The fleet manager computes stagger slots but has no collision detection, no re-stagger logic, and no collision rate metric. Needs: (1) per-link-pair collision counter in ingestion/signal processing path, (2) collision rate aggregation in fleet manager, (3) adaptive re-stagger trigger.

title: API: GET /api/doctor — pre-flight configuration diagnostic
description:
## Goal
Add a GET /api/doctor endpoint that diagnoses common misconfiguration before the user concludes the system is broken. Complements /healthz (runtime state) with pre-flight checks (configuration correctness).

## Endpoint

GET /api/doctor
- Requires session cookie (same as all /api/* endpoints)
- Returns 200 with a JSON report regardless of check results (HTTP status reflects reachability, not check results)

## Checks to run

| Check | Pass condition | Fail message |
|---|---|---|
| data_dir_writable | /data is writable and has >100 MB free | 'Data directory not writable' or 'Disk space low: Nf MB free' |
| db_integrity | PRAGMA integrity_check returns 'ok' | 'SQLite integrity check failed' |
| firmware_dir | /firmware contains at least one *.bin file | 'No firmware binaries found — OTA updates unavailable' |
| mdns_binding | mDNS service is registered (or SPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED=false) | 'mDNS not advertising — nodes cannot auto-discover mothership' |
| mqtt_reachable | If SPAXEL_MQTT_BROKER is set: TCP connect to broker succeeds within 3s | 'MQTT broker unreachable: <broker_url>' |
| ntp_reachable | UDP ping to SPAXEL_NTP_SERVER:123 resolves within 3s | 'NTP server unreachable — node clock sync may fail' |
| install_secret | install_secret row exists in auth table | 'Installation secret missing — re-run container to regenerate' |
| pin_configured | pin_bcrypt is non-null in auth table | 'Dashboard PIN not configured — run first-time setup' |
| node_token_consistency | All nodes in registry have non-null node_token | 'N nodes missing auth tokens — re-provision via Web Serial' |

## Response format

{
  'checks': [
    {'name': 'db_integrity', 'status': 'ok', 'message': null},
    {'name': 'mqtt_reachable', 'status': 'warn', 'message': 'MQTT broker unreachable: mqtt://ha.local:1883'},
    {'name': 'firmware_dir', 'status': 'error', 'message': 'No firmware binaries found'}
  ],
  'overall': 'warn',   // 'ok' | 'warn' | 'error' (worst of all checks)
  'checked_at': '2024-03-15T07:00:00Z'
}

Status levels: 'ok' (pass), 'warn' (degraded but functional), 'error' (action required).

## Dashboard integration
- Command palette: 'doctor' → calls /api/doctor, shows results inline
- Guided troubleshooting (Component 36): 'Node offline' flow links to 'Run diagnostics' which calls /api/doctor
- /healthz already covers runtime health; /api/doctor covers configuration health — keep them separate

## Acceptance
- GET /api/doctor returns 200 with all checks when fully configured
- Reports 'firmware_dir: error' when /firmware is empty
- Reports 'mqtt_reachable: warn' when MQTT broker env is set but broker is unreachable
- Unit tests cover each check in isolation with mocked dependencies

title: Add host test asserting default-placement sim produces non-zero fusion peaks
description:
Child of bf-18yn umbrella (final child). Depends on child 3. This is the bf-18yn acceptance-criteria test.

Scope:
- Add a sim/host (or package) test that, using only the DEFAULT node placement (no manual positioning), constructs/seeds the 3D fusion engine and asserts the accumulation grid produces non-zero peaks, i.e. len(blobs) > 0 or grid max above threshold, closing the bf-4q5w symptom. Extend the bf-6s3d fleet->engine invariant test or add a sibling.

Acceptance criteria:
- Test passes with default placement, and fails (or is skipped with a clear reason) if nodes collapse to (0,0,1).
- go build ./... passes; existing simulator and fusion tests still pass.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Implemented and pushed (commit 1df0bfa, on origin/main).

Added two sibling tests to TestEngine_SeedNodePositions (bf-6s3d) in mothership/internal/fusion/fusion_test.go:

1. TestEngine_DefaultPlacementProducesPeaks — the bf-18yn acceptance test / bf-4q5w fix. Seeds the 3D fusion engine using ONLY the default placement (simulator.DefaultNodePositions, the spread geometry a freshly-onboarded virtual/sim fleet gets with no manual positioning — bf-3fr6/bf-xrej), drives a synthetic walker through the room centre, and asserts the accumulation grid produces non-zero peaks (len(blobs) > 0 OR gridMax above threshold) across default placements of 2/3/4/6 nodes. assertPlacementNotCollapsed fails loudly if placement ever collapses to (0,0,1).

2. TestEngine_CoLocatedOriginYieldsNoPeaks — counter-example pinning the bf-4q5w symptom: nodes left at the (0,0,1) DB default are co-located, every link is degenerate, the grid stays at zero, and Fuse emits 0 blobs. Proves the non-zero-peak assertion is meaningful, not trivially satisfiable.

Acceptance criteria met: passes with default placement; co-located (0,0,1) collapse yields no peaks (documented). go build ./... / go vet ./... / go test ./... all green across mothership, cmd/sim, test/acceptance.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Closed bf-18yn acceptance test (commit 1df0bfa, pushed to origin/main).

TestEngine_DefaultPlacementProducesPeaks: seeds the 3D fusion engine using ONLY the default node placement — simulator.DefaultNodePositions, the spread geometry a freshly-onboarded virtual/sim fleet receives with no manual positioning (bf-3fr6/bf-xrej). Runs over 2/3/4/6-node default placements, drives a synthetic walker (buildSyntheticLinks) through the room centre, and asserts len(blobs) > 0 OR gridMax above threshold. assertPlacementNotCollapsed fails loudly if default placement ever collapses to the co-located (0,0,1) DB default (the bf-4q5w root cause).

TestEngine_CoLocatedOriginYieldsNoPeaks: counter-example pinning bf-4q5w — nodes left collapsed at (0,0,1) are co-located, every link is degenerate, the grid stays at zero, and Fuse emits 0 blobs. Proves the non-zero-peak assertion is meaningful, not trivially satisfiable.

Sibling to bf-6s3d's TestEngine_SeedNodePositions (which locks the seeding invariant; this locks its downstream consequence).

Acceptance verified: go build/vet/test ./... pass across all three go.work modules (mothership incl. internal/fusion + internal/simulator, cmd/sim, test/acceptance). Targeted run: DefaultPlacement subtests nodes=2/3/4/6 PASS, CoLocatedOriginYieldsNoPeaks PASS, SeedNodePositions PASS.

title: Fix the dead token path: bridge the sim token into hello.Token
description:
Second link of the bf-4iewr split (depends on the root-cause finding). The token the sim provisions never reaches the validator — make the token path REAL so a sim node presenting a valid token is accepted even when the migration window is closed.

## Problem (confirmed by the root-cause child)
- Validator reads only `hello.Token` (`internal/ingestion/server.go:513`).
- Sim sends its token only as the `X-Spaxel-Token` HTTP header (`cmd/sim/main.go:633`), never in the hello body, and the mothership never reads that header. So `hello.Token` is always "" and every node falls through to the migration-window branch.

## Scope
Pick ONE bridging approach and implement it (justify the choice in the PR/comment):
- (A) Server-side: in the `/ws/node` upgrade handler, read `X-Spaxel-Token` from the WS handshake request and inject it into the parsed hello (`nc.Hello.Token` / the value passed to the validator) before the validation check at server.go:506-530; OR
- (B) Client-side: have the sim include a `"token"` field in the hello JSON body (`cmd/sim/main.go` hello map ~640, and `cmd/sim/scenario.go`).

Prefer the option that keeps real ESP32 firmware behavior unchanged (firmware today sends token in hello — verify against `firmware/main/websocket.c`). Do not weaken the validator.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A node presenting a VALID provisioned token is accepted and marked paired (not Unpaired) even when `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0` (window closed) — proven by a test.
- [ ] A node presenting an INVALID/empty token is still REJECTED when the window is closed (regression guard; do not break rejection).
- [ ] Test (unit or e2e) exercises the bridge end-to-end through `ValidateToken` (`internal/provisioning/server.go:135`, HMAC-SHA256(installSecret, mac)).

title: Send node position in spaxel-sim hello and persist it on registration
description:
Child of bf-18yn umbrella. Depends on the geometry helper (child 1).

Problem: cmd/sim/main.go and mothership/cmd/sim/main.go compute good corner geometry in generateNodePositions/createVirtualNodes, but the hello message sent on WebSocket connect (connectNodes) does NOT include position. So the mothership never learns the sim node location and the fleet registry/DB row is left at the schema default (0,0,1), co-located with every other node.

Scope:
- Add the node computed position (X,Y,Z) to the hello JSON message in both cmd/sim/main.go and mothership/cmd/sim/main.go, sourced from createVirtualNodes (or child 1 helper).
- In the mothership node-registration/hello handler, accept the position and store it via the registry so the node DB row is NOT left at (0,0,1). Ensure it flows to the fusion engine via the existing connect/register sink wired in bf-3p6g (manager.go OnNodeConnected -> nodePositionSink -> fusionEngine.SetNodePosition).

Acceptance criteria:
- A spaxel-sim node connecting with a position results in a fleet-registry/DB node row whose pos differs from (0,0,1) and matches the sent coordinates.
- go build ./... passes; registration-path tests updated or added.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Implemented and verified: both sim CLIs (cmd/sim and mothership/cmd/sim) announce pos_x/pos_y/pos_z in the hello handshake, sourced from createVirtualNodes corner geometry. HelloMessage gained PosX/PosY/PosZ as *float64 (nil = not announced, so a real ESP32 preserves any user-placed position). The connect/register path (server.HandleNodeWS -> FleetNotifier.OnNodeConnected -> Manager) carries the announced position through; the Manager persists it via registry.SetNodePosition only when all three axes are present, then forwards it to the fusion engine through the bf-3p6g sink (GetNodePosition -> ForwardNodePosition -> fusionEngine.SetNodePosition). Acceptance criteria met: TestManagerOnNodeConnectedPersistsHelloPosition asserts a node connecting with an announced position yields a registry/DB row whose pos differs from (0,0,1) and matches the sent coords, and that the coords reach the fusion engine. go build ./..., go vet ./..., and go test ./... (50 packages) all pass. Commit d048987 pushed to origin/main.

title: Guided troubleshooting (Component 36)
description:
## Goal
When system detects that user might be struggling or detection quality has degraded, proactively offer contextual help — but never when things are working well.

## Trigger conditions and responses:

Detection quality drops:
- Condition: Zone-level detection quality below 60% for >24 hours
- Banner in timeline and 3D view: 'Detection in the kitchen has been less reliable this week. Want me to help diagnose?'
- Guided flow: Check node connectivity → show link health with explainability → suggest node repositioning → offer re-baseline → 'Still not right? Try adding a node here [highlighted position]'

Repeated setting changes:
- Condition: Same settings key modified 3+ times within 60-minute sliding window (qualifying keys: delta_rms_threshold, breathing_sensitivity, tau_s, fresnel_decay, n_subcarriers)
- Tracking: per-key edit counter in memory, resets after 60 min inactivity
- Trigger: when counter reaches 3, set hint_pending flag
- Frontend: show non-intrusive banner: 'You've adjusted the detection threshold several times. Would you like me to show you what the system is seeing?' with [Show me] and [×] dismiss
- [Show me]: opens time-travel to most recent detection event before first edit, with explainability overlay pre-activated
- Cooldown: 24 hours after hint is shown

Node offline:
- Condition: Any node offline for >2 hours
- Timeline event with expandable troubleshooting steps

First-time feature discovery:
- Condition: User opens feature panel for first time
- Brief, non-intrusive tooltip (not modal): 'Draw a box around an area, then choose what happens when someone enters or leaves. [Got it]'
- Shown once, never repeated

After false positive feedback:
- Inline response in timeline: 'Got it. I've slightly raised the detection threshold for the contributing links. If this keeps happening at this time of day, my hourly baseline will adapt within a few days.'

After successful calibration:
- Positive reinforcement: 'Re-baseline complete. Detection quality in the kitchen improved from 64% to 89%.'

## Design principles
- Reactive, not proactive: help appears only when something seems wrong or when user is clearly exploring
- Dismissible in one tap: never blocks UI
- Never repeats after dismissal (stored in localStorage)
- Always explains what will happen next
- Never condescending: assumes user is intelligent but may not know CSI physics

## Acceptance
- Detection quality drop triggers helpful banner
- Repeated setting changes trigger hint
- Node offline shows troubleshooting steps
- First-time feature discovery shows tooltip once
- Feedback responses are helpful
- Calibration success shows positive reinforcement

title: Component 24: Fleet-level sentinel link coordination (adaptive sensing idle mode)
description:
The ratecontrol.go implements per-node adaptive rate (active: 20-50 Hz, idle: 2 Hz) but is missing the fleet-level coordination described in plan.md Component 24.

The plan specifies:
- When ALL zones are idle, designate one sentinel link per zone at 5 Hz; all other links drop to 1 Hz
- When activity detected in one zone, ramp that zone to full rate AND adjacent zones to 5 Hz (adjacency exists but only via OnMotionHint, not zone-level)
- Prediction engine can preemptively ramp zones before predicted arrivals

What exists in ratecontrol.go:
- Per-node state machine: Active (20 Hz) / Idle (2 Hz)
- OnMotionHint: preemptively ramps adjacent nodes to full rate
- OnMotionState: transitions node between active/idle
- SetAdjacentNodesFn: adjacency function for adjacent-node ramping

What is missing:
1. Zone-level idle detection: when all nodes in a zone have been idle, designate one sentinel link per zone at 5 Hz and drop all others to 1 Hz
2. Fleet-wide idle state: when all zones are idle, only sentinel links at 5 Hz run
3. Prediction-engine-driven preemptive zone ramp: RateController needs a RampZone(zoneID) method that the prediction engine can call when P(arrival in zone) > threshold
4. The 1 Hz floor rate (current code uses 2 Hz idle; plan says 1 Hz for all-idle fleet state)

Scope: Add zone-aware idle coordination to RateController. Add RampZone(zoneID) interface for prediction engine wiring. Adjust idle rate to 1 Hz for fully-idle fleet.

title: Confirm sim sends token as header only and mothership ignores it
description:
Second link of the bf-34lwt split. Confirm the sim-side token-sending path and the mothership's failure to read it. This proves the 'supply valid tokens' option is currently DEAD — the core of the root cause.

## Scope (cite file:line)
3. The sim sends its provisioned token ONLY as the `X-Spaxel-Token` HTTP header (`cmd/sim/main.go`, also `cmd/sim/scenario.go`) and does NOT include a `token` field in the hello JSON body (hello map at cmd/sim/main.go).
4. The mothership NEVER reads that header — `grep -rn 'X-Spaxel-Token\|Header.Get' cmd/mothership/ internal/` returns no WS-path hit. Consequence: `hello.Token` is always "" for sim nodes → `tokenOK` always false.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Quoted file:line(s) showing the sim sets only the `X-Spaxel-Token` header and that the hello JSON map has no `token` field.
- [ ] Grep output (or equivalent) demonstrating no WS-path read of `X-Spaxel-Token` in `cmd/mothership/` or `internal/`.
- [ ] Explicit statement: the token-supply path is currently dead (header ignored → hello.Token empty → tokenOK always false).

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Confirmed (docs/notes/token-supply-path-dead-confirm.md, commit 4dcc06a): the token-supply path is currently DEAD. (1) Sim supplies its token ONLY as the X-Spaxel-Token HTTP header on the WS dial — cmd/sim/main.go:313, mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:633, mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:318. (2) The hello JSON map has NO token field — cmd/sim/main.go:348-359, mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:652-665, mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:331-342. (3) The mothership never reads that header on the WS path — grep 'X-Spaxel-Token' mothership/ (excluding _test.go and cmd/sim/) returns zero hits; HandleNodeWS (server.go:455-469) passes r straight to Upgrade. Consequence: header ignored -> hello.Token (message.go:22) empty -> tokenOK (server.go:513) always false. Second link of the bf-34lwt split; pairs with bf-5ig3e.

title: Simulator: ray-based propagation engine (internal/sim/propagation.go — direct path + first-order reflections)
Investigate which fusion engine feeds the live blob loop and IO-6 gate
description:
Prerequisite investigation for the bf-3gw1 umbrella. The live 10Hz loop in mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:1867 calls pm.GetTrackedBlobs(), but grep confirms mothership/internal/fusion.NewEngine (3D Fresnel engine, fusion.go:93) is NEVER constructed in non-test code. Determine definitively which engine produces the blobs consumed by the live loop and the IO-6 hard-gate.

Tasks:
- Trace pm (processing manager) and GetTrackedBlobs() to the engine that actually emits tracked blobs in the live path.
- Confirm whether internal/fusion.Engine is the intended blob source, or whether blobs come from a different engine (e.g. the localization tracker).
- Find/confirm any existing non-test construction site of internal/fusion.NewEngine (expected: none).
- Record the finding as a bead comment: engine package, construction site (or 'none - must be added'), and the exact line where the live loop reads blobs.

Acceptance criteria:
- A documented determination (bead comment) naming the blob-producing engine with file:line references.
- States explicitly whether internal/fusion.NewEngine must be newly constructed in non-test code.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
INVESTIGATION COMPLETE (bf-2fz8). Determination of which fusion engine feeds the live blob loop + IO-6 gate:

BLOB SOURCE: NONE. No engine currently produces tracked blobs for the live path.
- pm.trackedBlobs (internal/signal/processor.go:227) is always nil: SetTrackedBlobs (processor.go:625) has ZERO callers.
- GetTrackedBlobs() is read by: the live 10Hz loop (cmd/mothership/main.go:1866), /api/blobs (main.go:4056), anomalyPositionAdapter (main.go:4840), and API wrappers (internal/api/status.go:84, internal/api/tracks.go:99) — ALL see zero blobs. IO-6 ('walker produces a tracked blob') cannot pass.

ENGINE INVENTORY:
- internal/fusion.NewEngine (3D Fresnel, internal/fusion/fusion.go:93) = the INTENDED blob producer, but NEVER constructed in non-test code. The package is imported by exactly one file, internal/localizer/fusion/timing_budget_test.go (a test); the only NewEngine call sites are that test and internal/fusion/fusion_test.go.
- internal/localization.NewEngine (2D Fresnel, internal/localization/fusion.go:53) = IS live, via NewSelfImprovingLocalizer (main.go:1005), runs Fuse (main.go:2593), but the returned *FusionResult is DISCARDED — nothing reads .Peaks, and no signal.TrackedBlob literal exists outside test code.

CONCLUSION: internal/fusion.NewEngine MUST be newly constructed in non-test code and wired (Fuse -> TrackedBlob -> SetTrackedBlobs), OR the existing localization.Engine FusionResult.Peaks must be converted to TrackedBlobs and stored via SetTrackedBlobs. Also recorded in code as a doc comment on SetTrackedBlobs (commit a7ec9d2).

comments.author: cli
comments.text:

FINDING: NO engine currently feeds the live blob loop. pm.trackedBlobs is always nil.

- Blob source determination: SetTrackedBlobs (internal/signal/processor.go:631) is the ONLY writer of pm.trackedBlobs and has ZERO callers -> GetTrackedBlobs() always returns nil -> IO-6 cannot pass.
- Exact live-loop read line: cmd/mothership/main.go:1866 (blobs := pm.GetTrackedBlobs()). Other readers: /api/blobs main.go:4056, anomalyPositionAdapter main.go:4840, internal/api/status.go:84, internal/api/tracks.go:99.
- internal/fusion.NewEngine (3D Fresnel, internal/fusion/fusion.go:93): construction site = NONE in non-test code. The internal/fusion package is imported by ZERO non-test files; only NewEngine call sites are internal/localizer/fusion/timing_budget_test.go and internal/fusion/fusion_test.go.
- internal/localization.NewEngine (2D Fresnel, internal/localization/fusion.go:53) IS constructed live (NewSelfImprovingLocalizer main.go:1005, Fuse main.go:2593) but the returned *FusionResult is DISCARDED -> nothing reads .Peaks; only GetLearnedWeights() consumed (main.go:2599). No signal.TrackedBlob literal outside tests (automation.TrackedBlob at main.go:2111 is a distinct type).

title: Confirm migration window is the only thing accepting sim nodes
description:
Third link of the bf-34lwt split. Confirm that the migration window — not tokens — is what accepts tokenless sim nodes, and pin down exactly when REJECT fires. This proves current 'no reject' is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix.

## Scope (cite file:line)
5. The only thing accepting sim nodes is the migration window: `internal/ingestion/server.go`. MigrationWindowHours DEFAULTS TO 24 (`internal/config/config.go`), so a fresh boot opens a 24h window → sim nodes accepted as Unpaired, no reject (matches PROGRESS.md bf-3hji).
6. Therefore REJECT fires ONLY when the window is closed: `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0` (deadline never set → IsZero() → reject branch), OR uptime > 24h. In those cases every sim node logs 'missing token' → `sendReject(conn, 'invalid_token')` → disconnect.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Quoted file:line(s) showing MigrationWindowHours default = 24 and the migration-deadline acceptance branch.
- [ ] Quoted file:line of the reject branch (window closed / IsZero()) and the `sendReject(...,'invalid_token')` call.
- [ ] Explicit statement: current 'no reject' is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix — REJECT returns when window=0 or uptime>24h.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
DONE — committed docs(bf-2hdbg) 8116dc8, pushed to origin main (PROGRESS.md only; unrelated e2e_test.go/.beads bookkeeping left uncommitted).

Confirmed against current source that the migration window — not tokens — is the sole acceptor of tokenless sim nodes:

- config.go:139 MigrationWindowHours defaults to 24 (config.go:43 '0 = disabled'); main.go:4495-4497 installs deadline=now+24h only when >0, so a fresh boot opens the window.
- server.go:510-518 acceptance branch gates purely on server.go:515 (!deadline.IsZero() && time.Now().Before(deadline)); tokenOK (server.go:513) is irrelevant — tokenless node admitted as Unpaired.
- server.go:519-526 reject branch (else of 515) → server.go:525 sendReject(conn,"invalid_token") + conn.Close(); sendReject emits {"type":"reject","reason":"invalid_token"} (server.go:841-845).

Conclusion: current 'no reject' is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix. REJECT returns when SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 (deadline never set → IsZero() → reject) OR uptime > 24h (Before(deadline) false → reject). Real fix is token/supply side.

Acceptance criteria all met in PROGRESS.md §bf-2hdbg (lines 752-855). go vet + build + internal unit tests clean (docs change touches no Go code).

title: Add identity fields to explainability.BlobSnapshot type
description:
## Objective
Add the three canonical identity fields — PersonName, AssignedColor, IdentityResolved — to the `explainability.BlobSnapshot` struct type, which currently lacks them. This is the Tier-1 #2 type gap from the bf-1q3m/bf-5cgc handoff.

## Scope (grounded in notes/bf-1q3m-consolidated.md §6 Tier-1 #2)

- Add `PersonName string`, `AssignedColor string`, `IdentityResolved *bool` with JSON tags matching the dashboard camelCase keys (`person_name`, `assigned_color`, `identity_resolved`), mirroring exactly how commit 1446ccf added them to signal.TrackedBlob / tracker.Blob / tracking.Blob / automation.TrackedBlob.
- The production creation literal at `cmd/mothership/main.go:2206` needs NO change — Go zero values serialize to undefined in JS via omitempty. Fields-as-undefined, consistent with 1446ccf + 248d0e0.
- OUT OF SCOPE (do not do here — it is a separate downstream bead): populating these fields from the `ble.IdentityMatcher` sidecar, and folding the parallel `identityMap` (main.go:2216) into the snapshot. This child adds field DECLARATIONS only, left at zero values.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PersonName, AssignedColor, IdentityResolved added to explainability.BlobSnapshot (handler.go:95) with correct JSON tags
- [ ] Fields left at zero values at the main.go:2206 literal — NO populate/sidecar logic added
- [ ] gofmt clean; `go vet ./internal/explainability/...` clean; `go test ./internal/explainability/...` passes

title: Activity timeline (Component 27)
description:
## Goal
Implement universal event stream timeline that serves as primary navigation for time and space.

## Scope
- Event types: detections, zone transitions, portal crossings, automation triggers, alerts (fall/anomaly/security), system events (node online/offline, OTA, baseline changes), learning milestones
- Tap any event → 3D view jumps to that exact moment via time-travel
- Inline actions per event: thumbs up/down (feedback), 'Why?' (explainability), create automation from event
- Filters: By person, by zone, by event type, by time range (combinable)
- Search: Natural language queries like 'kitchen occupied after midnight last week'
- Scroll up = go back in time. Open dashboard after being away → scroll up to see everything that happened

## Location
dashboard/static/js/timeline.js (new module)
internal/api/events.go (GET /api/events endpoint already exists)

## Acceptance
- Timeline sidebar in expert mode shows all events in scrollable stream
- Simple mode: timeline IS the main view as activity feed, with room cards above it
- Tap event → 3D scene shows state at that moment
- Search filters events correctly
- FTS5 index on events table for natural language search
close_reason:
Component 27 - Activity Timeline already fully implemented.

Timeline sidebar (expert mode): sidebar-timeline.js provides collapsible panel with scrollable events, category checkboxes, person/zone filters, date range selector, text search with fuzzy matching, cursor pagination, virtualization for 1000+ events, WebSocket real-time updates.

Simple mode activity feed: simple.html imports timeline.js as ES6 module; timeline is main view below room cards.

Tap-to-time-travel: Both timeline.js and sidebar-timeline.js implement handleSeek() calling SpaxelReplay.jumpToTime() to jump 3D view to event timestamp.

Search: Fuzzy matching client-side + FTS5 server-side via /api/events?q= parameter.

FTS5 index: events.go creates events_fts virtual table with triggers for automatic full-text indexing.

All acceptance criteria met.

title: Ambient dashboard mode (Component 31)
description:
## Goal
Dedicated display mode for wall-mounted tablets or always-on screens. Served at /ambient as separate lightweight route.

## Scope
- Simplified, stylized top-down floor plan — clean lines, soft rounded corners, no UI chrome
- People appear as softly glowing colored circles (BLE-identified) or neutral dots (unknown), with names
- Room labels show subtle occupancy: 'Kitchen · Alice' or 'Bedroom · Empty'
- Smooth, calm animations: dots drift with interpolated positions, no jitter, no snapping
- No toolbar, no buttons, no panels — just floor plan, people, small status line
- Time-of-day awareness: morning (bright/cool), day (neutral), evening (warm/amber), night (very dim, minimal)
- Adaptive behavior: house empty 30+ min → screen goes fully dark, 'All secure' in tiny text
- Alert event: entire display transitions to alert mode with pulsing red border, large text, action buttons
- Morning briefing integration: when first person detected, display briefly shows briefing text before fading to ambient

## Implementation
/ambient route serving lightweight HTML page
No Three.js — use Canvas 2D or SVG for minimal resource usage
WebSocket receives same dashboard feed but only uses blob positions, zone counts, alerts
<30 MB RAM, <5% CPU on 2018 iPad

## Acceptance
- Ambient mode runs unattended on wall-mounted tablet for 7+ days
- Time-of-day palette transitions smoothly
- Alert mode breaks the calm appropriately
- Morning briefing displays on first detection
- Resource usage: <30 MB RAM, <5% CPU

title: Provision ESP32 node: set WiFi creds + mothership URL
description:
After bf-4gbf (ESP32 flashed and boots successfully):

The freshly flashed node will start a captive portal AP named spaxel-XXXX (last 4 of MAC).

Steps:
1. Connect phone or laptop to the "spaxel-XXXX" WiFi AP (no password)
2. Browser redirects to 192.168.4.1 captive portal
3. Enter WiFi SSID and password for the home network
4. Enter mothership URL: spaxel.ardenone.com (port 443, WSS)
   The firmware will derive ws://... internally — enter just the hostname.
5. Submit — node writes creds to NVS, reboots
6. Node should now connect to home WiFi and then discover mothership via:
   - mDNS: _spaxel._tcp.local (disabled in prod; mothership is remote)
   - Fallback: ms_ip NVS key → use the IP/hostname provisioned in step 4
7. Verify node appears in spaxel.ardenone.com fleet dashboard as CONNECTED
   (node sends hello: {type:hello, mac, firmware_version, capabilities})

Note: SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=168 so the node can connect without a
provisioned token for 7 days. No token management needed for first node.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Cannot complete via software agent — this is a hardware-validation task.

REQUIREMENTS A SOFTWARE AGENT LACKS:
- A physical ESP32-S3 dev board, freshly flashed and booting (prerequisite bf-4gbf).
- A human's phone/laptop to join the spaxel-XXXX captive-portal AP, type the home WiFi SSID/password + hostname 'spaxel.ardenone.com' into the 192.168.4.1 form, submit, and verify the node shows CONNECTED on the dashboard.

BLOCKED BY PREREQUISITE: bf-4gbf (Re-flash ESP32 via Web Serial) is still Status=pending and itself needs a human + USB-connected board. No flashed node exists yet to provision.

NO CODE TO WRITE: The entire provisioning path is already implemented in firmware/:
- Captive portal: wifi_start_captive_portal() in firmware/main/wifi.c (AP + DNS hijack to 192.168.4.1 + HTML form with ms_ip field, NVS write at wifi.c:399-400)
- Serial provisioning JSON: firmware/main/provision.c:163-166 (writes NVS_KEY_MS_IP / NVS_KEY_MS_IP_PROV)
- Boot fallback to provisioned mothership host: firmware/main/main.c:244-245 (ms_ip_prov)
- Migration window (SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=168) allows first connect without a token.

SERVER VERIFIED UP: https://spaxel.ardenone.com/healthz returns HTTP 200 (Cloudflare-fronted). Once a board is flashed + provisioned by a human, the node should appear CONNECTED.

ACTION: Left OPEN per task rules (do-not-close-when-incomplete). Needs a human + physical ESP32-S3 board. Same class as bf-4gbf / bf-4eal.

2. No ESP32 hardware on this host (no /dev/ttyUSB* or /dev/ttyACM* devices) — this is a remote Hetzner server with no board attached.
3. Steps 1-3 are inherently physical + require human-only data: a human must join the spaxel-XXXX WiFi AP, open the 192.168.4.1 captive portal in a browser, and type the HOME WiFi SSID/password. Those credentials are not in the repo and must not be guessed.

Needs: a human with (a) the ESP32-S3 board physically present, (b) bf-4gbf done first so the node boots into its captive portal, and (c) the home WiFi SSID/password. Leaving OPEN per the auto-retry policy — do not close until a human performs the captive-portal provisioning and the node shows CONNECTED at spaxel.ardenone.com.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
NOT CLOSEABLE by agent (still needs human + physical ESP32-S3 + home WiFi creds). Two NEW items vs comments 36-41:

(1) NETWORK CORRECTION — comment 39's 'TLS gap blocks step 7' is FALSE. Probed live: ws://spaxel.ardenone.com:8080/ws/node returns HTTP 101 (WebSocket upgrade succeeds through Cloudflare, which proxies :8080 as plain HTTP alongside :443). So the firmware's hardcoded ws://<host>:8080 path (websocket.c:107, ms_port default 8080) works end-to-end. A provisioned node WILL send hello and show CONNECTED — NO firmware wss/TLS work is a prerequisite for physical provisioning to succeed. (The bead's 'port 443 WSS' wording is misleading; in practice Cloudflare exposes the node-facing endpoint on :8080 plain-WS.) healthz on :8080 returns the real mothership JSON: version 0.1.358, nodes_online 0, db ok.

(2) ACTION to stop the retry loop — this bead was dispatched to agents 6x (comments 36-41), each reaching the same not-completable conclusion because prerequisite bf-4gbf (physically flash the board) is still pending. Added a blocking dependency: 'br dep add bf-2po1 bf-4gbf' (bf-2po1 depends-on bf-4gbf). Verified bf-2po1 is now excluded from 'br ready' while bf-4gbf is open. Once a human completes bf-4gbf (board flashed + booting into the spaxel-XXXX captive portal), bf-2po1 becomes ready and the steps are manual: join AP -> 192.168.4.1 -> enter home WiFi SSID/pass + 'spaxel.ardenone.com' -> verify CONNECTED. Left OPEN per do-not-close rule.

title: CSI simulator (spaxel-sim CLI)
description:
## Goal
Go CLI tool that opens WebSocket connections as virtual nodes and sends synthetic CSI binary frames for development/testing without hardware.

## CLI Interface
spaxel-sim --mothership ws://localhost:8080/ws/node --token <node_token> --nodes 4 --walkers 1 --rate 20 --duration 60s --ble --seed 42 --space '6x5x2.5'

## Synthetic CSI Generation
- Each virtual node has fixed position (corners, evenly distributed)
- Each walker follows random walk: Gaussian velocity updates (σ=0.3 m/s per axis per 50ms), reflected at walls
- For each TX→RX link pair at each tick: compute amplitude and phase using propagation model (path-loss + wall penetration + reflection)
- Inject Gaussian noise: amplitude_noisy[k] = amplitude × (1 + N(0, 0.05))
- Serialize into 24-byte binary frame format with n_sub=64
- rssi = clamp(-30 - path_loss_dB, -90, -30), noise_floor = -95

## Location
cmd/sim/main.go (new package)

## Acceptance
- Simulator exits non-zero if it receives {type:'reject'} from mothership
- Prints per-second frame counts and blob count (from GET /api/blobs poll)
- Integration test: run simulator for 30s, assert blob count > 0
- --ble flag also sends simulated BLE advertisements every 5s
close_reason:
Implemented CSI simulator CLI tool (spaxel-sim). The simulator connects to a running mothership via WebSocket and streams synthetic CSI data for testing without hardware.

Key features:
- CLI flags: --mothership, --token, --nodes, --walkers, --rate, --duration, --ble, --seed, --space
- Synthetic CSI generation using Fresnel zone propagation model
- Virtual nodes at fixed positions (corners, evenly distributed)
- Walker random walk with Gaussian velocity updates (σ=0.3 m/s per axis per 50ms)
- Wall reflection at space boundaries
- 24-byte binary frame format with n_sub=64
- RSSI clamped to [-90, -30] dBm, noise_floor=-95
- Exits non-zero on {type:'reject'} from mothership
- Prints per-second frame counts and blob count (from GET /api/blobs poll)
- --ble flag sends simulated BLE advertisements every 5s

Binary built and copied to mothership directory for integration tests.
All tests pass (go test ./... && go vet ./...).

title: Component 5: Self-healing fleet — before/after coverage overlay in dashboard
description:
The fleet manager self-healing logic (fleet/manager.go selfHeal()) correctly re-optimizes roles and pushes new config to nodes when a node goes offline. However the dashboard visualization described in plan.md Component 12 is partially missing.

What the plan requires:
- Dashboard shows a before/after coverage comparison overlay when self-healing fires: Node kitchen-ceiling went offline. Coverage in kitchen corridor degraded from excellent to fair. 4 links lost, 8 remaining.
- When node reconnects: Node kitchen-ceiling back online. Full coverage restored.
- Affected areas highlighted in red in the 3D view (not just the node changing to red status dot)

What exists:
- Node status color change (green/yellow/red) on disconnect
- selfHeal() re-optimizes roles and pushes config
- Timeline events for node online/offline

What is missing:
- Before/after GDOP comparison when self-heal fires: compute coverage with N nodes vs N-1 nodes, diff
- Overlay in 3D view highlighting zones with degraded coverage (ground plane zones turn amber/red)
- WebSocket message type for coverage-degradation event with affected-zones payload
- Frontend handling in viz3d.js to show the coverage degradation overlay and dismiss after recovery

This is distinct from the GDOP overlay in setup mode — it fires automatically on node loss, not on manual placement.

title: Firmware host-based unit tests: nvs/csi/serial_prov via idf.py test --target linux
description: Testing Strategy requires 3 firmware modules tested via idf.py test --target linux (no hardware needed): (1) nvs schema migration test, (2) csi binary frame serialization roundtrip, (3) serial_prov JSON parser fuzz. No bead (open or closed) covers this. Implement the host test targets in the ESP-IDF component CMakeLists and wire them into CI.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Host-test intent satisfied by the committed gcc harness under firmware/test/ (build-out of decision spike bf-21t), NOT idf.py test --target linux. The idf.py --target linux mechanism named in this bead was explicitly REJECTED (decision record docs/notes/firmware-host-test-approach.md): firmware/main builds as ONE idf_component_register whose REQUIRES names esp_wifi/bt/driver -- three components with no host build -- so the whole component is unhostable; even nvs_migration.c (hostable in isolation) is blocked because main is one component. Building the idf.py path would require risky #ifdef-guarding of production firmware and create a second test mechanism (the decision record warns against exactly this). The gcc harness delivers exactly what the Testing Strategy requires, hardware-free: (1) nvs schema migration test_nvs_migration.c (fresh-install v1, no-downgrade guard, forward-migration dispatch, v1->v2 rename+default -- 8 tests); (2) csi binary frame serialization roundtrip test_csi_frame.c (24-byte header roundtrip, little-endian timestamp, signed-RSSI reinterpretation, I/Q payload, n_sub=0 probe, ingestion-side validation -- 7 tests); (3) serial_prov JSON parser fuzz test_serial_prov.c (bounded recursive-descent decoder fuzzed across random byte streams + deep-nesting cap, valid/invalid/minimal payloads -- 12 tests incl. fuzz passes). Result: 29 passed, 0 failed of 29. CI gate: Dockerfile 'RUN make -C test test' (plain gcc, no IDF toolchain; runs before the ESP-IDF build, propagates non-zero exit). Cleanup this bead: gitignored firmware/test_apps/ -- leftover CMake/ninja build output from an abandoned idf.py linux experiment (no source files; root-owned Docker artifacts so left in place, but now gitignored so it cannot be committed). Verification: make -C firmware/test test -> 29/29; cd mothership && go test ./... -> PASS; go vet ./... -> clean. Pushed 4b0eaba..fbb86fb.

title: Component 33: Interactive Onboarding — Teach by Doing (5-step CSI physics wizard)
description:
The existing onboard.js has a basic 3-phase calibration wizard (walk/still/walk_through) but is missing the full Component 33 spec from plan.md.

What exists: 3-phase calibration (walk 30s, still 10s, walk-through 15s), live CSI waveform canvas, post-calibration card.

What is missing — 5 teaching steps with specific narration:

Step 1 (walk 30s): Copy reads I am listening to your WiFi signal. Walk across the room / See that? Your body just changed the WiFi signal — that is how I detect you. Amplitude spikes highlighted in real-time.

Step 2 (still 10s): A green baseline line fades in on chart. Copy reads This is your rooms baseline — the signal when nothing is moving. Any change from this means someone is here. Baseline captured automatically here, not via separate calibrate trigger.

Step 3 (walk through detection zone 15s): Fresnel zone ellipsoid between node and router lights up as translucent green in 3D scene. Copy reads Walk between your node and the router — through the green zone / That is the Fresnel zone — I am most sensitive along this path. Zone pulses brighter as user crosses.

Step 4 (let me find you 15s): Copy reads Walk somewhere and stop. I will try to locate you. Humanoid figure appears at estimated position with dotted accuracy radius. Copy reads Found you! I estimate you are about here. My accuracy is plus-or-minus 1 meter with this setup.

Step 5 (place your node — interactive): Coverage painting activates on ground plane inside wizard. Copy reads Now drag your node to where it actually is in the room. Watch the green coverage change. After placement shows coverage score percentage. Copy reads Nice! Your coverage score is N percent.

Key constraint: zero jargon — CSI, Fresnel, deltaRMS must never appear in wizard text.

Implementation: Steps 3-4 require viz3d.js integration; step 5 requires placement.js GDOP overlay inside wizard; step 4 polls GET /api/blobs for humanoid placement.

title: Investigate and document the REJECT/token root cause in the WS hello path
description:
First link of the bf-4iewr split. Write the definitive root-cause for the tokenless-sim REJECT failure mode so the remaining children have a precise target. The bead is currently PASSING-BY-ACCIDENT (no reject in a default boot) — this child explains exactly why, and exactly when it breaks.

Confirm and document each of the following against the current source (cite file:line):

## Scope
1. Validator is wired UNCONDITIONALLY: `cmd/mothership/main.go:4494` calls `ingestSrv.SetTokenValidator(provSrv.ValidateToken)` — there is no config/build gate, so "validator configured?" is always YES.
2. The validator reads `hello.Token` only: `internal/ingestion/server.go:513` (`tokenOK := hello.Token != "" && validator(hello.MAC, hello.Token)`).
3. The sim sends its provisioned token ONLY as the `X-Spaxel-Token` HTTP header (`cmd/sim/main.go:633`, also `cmd/sim/scenario.go:318`) and does NOT include a `token` field in the hello JSON body (hello map at cmd/sim/main.go ~640).
4. The mothership NEVER reads that header — `grep -rn 'X-Spaxel-Token\|Header.Get' cmd/mothership/ internal/` returns no WS-path hit. Consequence: `hello.Token` is always "" for sim nodes → `tokenOK` always false → the "supply valid tokens" option in the parent bead is currently NON-FUNCTIONAL.
5. The only thing accepting sim nodes is the migration window: `internal/ingestion/server.go:515`. MigrationWindowHours DEFAULTS TO 24 (`internal/config/config.go:139`), so a fresh boot opens a 24h window → sim nodes accepted as Unpaired, no reject (matches PROGRESS.md bf-3hji).
6. Therefore REJECT fires ONLY when the window is closed: `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0` (deadline never set -> IsZero() -> reject branch), OR uptime > 24h. In those cases every sim node logs "missing token" -> sendReject(conn, "invalid_token") -> disconnect.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A written root-cause finding posted as a comment on bf-4iewr, confirming points 1-6 with file:line citations.
- [ ] Explicit statement that current "no reject" is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix.
- [ ] Explicit statement that the token-supply path is currently dead (header ignored, hello.Token empty).

title: Acceptance scenario integration tests (AS-1 through AS-6)
description:
## Goal
Implement the 6 acceptance scenarios from plan §Acceptance Scenarios as verifiable integration tests in test/acceptance/.

## Scenarios to implement

AS-1: First-time setup in under 5 minutes
  - Start fresh mothership container, open /api/auth/setup, set PIN
  - Run spaxel-sim --nodes 1 (simulates provisioned node)
  - Assert: node appears in /api/nodes within 30s

AS-2: Person detected while walking

  - Poll /api/blobs every second
  - Assert: blob count > 0 for >80% of the run

AS-3: Fall alert fires correctly
  - spaxel-sim with a walker that drops Z rapidly (spike downward velocity, then stays at Z<0.5)
  - Assert: events table contains fall_alert within 15s of trigger
  - Assert: webhook endpoint (test HTTP server) receives POST

AS-4: BLE identity resolves to person name
  - Register a BLE device as 'Alice' via POST /api/ble/devices
  - spaxel-sim --ble (sends BLE reports for that address alongside blobs)
  - Assert: /api/blobs returns at least one blob with person='Alice'

AS-5: OTA update succeeds / rollback on bad firmware
  - Already partially covered by existing OTA rollback integration test
  - Extend to verify the VERIFIED badge path (valid firmware + node reconnects with new version)

AS-6: Replay shows recorded history
  - Run 60s of sim data
  - POST /api/replay/start with a 30s window
  - Assert: replay blobs are returned via WebSocket with replay:true flag

## Location
test/acceptance/*.go — one file per scenario, parallel to test/integration/

## Acceptance
All 6 scenarios pass in the Argo CI workflow using spaxel-sim as the test harness

title: API path mismatch: crowd flow at /api/analytics/flow vs plan spec /api/flow
description:

title: Construct blob-producing fusion engine in non-test code and integrate into live loop
description:
Depends on bf-2fz8 findings. Ensure the confirmed blob-producing fusion engine (expected: mothership/internal/fusion.Engine) is constructed in non-test code and wired into the live 10Hz loop in mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go.

Tasks:
- Construct the engine at startup (fusion.NewEngine with a Config reflecting room geometry: Width/Height/Depth/Origin) if it is currently test-only.
- Wire it so the live loop's blob source (the pm.GetTrackedBlobs() path at main.go:1867) actually derives blobs from this engine — feed it link motion via Fuse, or connect it as the tracker source, per the bf-2fz8 determination.
- Expose the engine instance at a scope where startup seeding (next child) can call SetNodePosition on it.

Acceptance criteria:
- The fusion engine is constructed in non-test (cmd/mothership) code and reachable from the live loop.
- The engine instance is retained at a scope where startup seeding can call SetNodePosition on it.
- go build ./... passes.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Implemented (commit 30a7ea3, pushed).

The 3D Fresnel engine (internal/fusion.NewEngine) was only ever built in tests (bf-2f8), so pm.trackedBlobs was always nil and every GetTrackedBlobs() reader saw zero blobs.

Wiring:
- fusion.NewEngine constructed at startup with a Config mirroring room geometry (Width/Height/Depth/Origin from the fleet room config). Declared as a function-local retained for the life of main (mirrors selfImprovingLocalizer) so the startup seeding loop and the follow-up node-seeding bead can call SetNodePosition, and the live-loop closure captures it to run Fuse each tick.
- Node registry seeded from the fleet at startup (without positioned nodes Fuse skips every link).
- Tracker goroutine now runs fusionEngine.Fuse(gatherFusionLinks(pm)) -> new blobTracker.track(result) -> pm.SetTrackedBlobs(blobs), replacing the dead pm.GetTrackedBlobs() read. blobTracker bridges the engine's stateless peaks to stateful sigproc.TrackedBlob IDs via greedy 1.0m nearest-neighbour association (plan.md Component 4) and derives velocity from the prior position of the same tracked ID.

Gates: go build ./... / go vet ./... / go test ./... all pass.

Instance is reachable at function scope for the next child's SetNodePosition seeding.

title: Add shared default-node geometry helper to the simulator package
description:
Child of bf-18yn umbrella. Foundation for giving default spaxel-sim nodes a spread, non-degenerate geometry.

Context: the prior runtime-propagation child (bf-3p6g) wires node PosX/PosY/PosZ into the 3D fusion engine (mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go seeds fusionEngine via fleetReg.GetAllNodes()), and bf-1tsm asserts the result. But every node defaults to (0,0,1) in the DB schema (mothership/internal/db/migrations.go pos_x/pos_y/pos_z), so all nodes are co-located and the Fresnel excess path |P-T|+|P-R|-|T-R| collapses toward 0, producing no blobs (core symptom in bf-4q5w).

Scope:
- Add a reusable helper in mothership/internal/simulator that returns spread-out default positions for N nodes across a zone consistent with roomWidth/roomDepth/roomHeight. Reuse/extend CornerPositions/SuggestedNodes in node.go, or add e.g. DefaultNodePositions(space, count) []Point. The standalone cmd/sim/main.go generateNodePositions already does corner placement; align defaults with it.

Acceptance criteria:
- Helper returns N distinct, in-bounds positions; no two nodes co-located; positions span the room (min and max differ on X and Y).
- Unit test asserts pairwise node distance > 0 for all pairs.
- go build ./... and go test ./mothership/internal/simulator/... pass.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Added DefaultNodePositions(space, count) to mothership/internal/simulator/node.go — the shared default-geometry source for spread, non-degenerate node seeding. Single node -> room center; two -> diagonal corners; >=3 -> ceil(sqrt(count)) grid fill (guarantees both X and Y span + all-distinct positions, since count>=3 touches >=2 cols and rows). Z alternates low/high bands for height diversity. Aligns with cmd/sim generateNodePositions. Added node_positions_test.go asserting: count/zero/negative handling, in-bounds (incl. offset-origin space), pairwise distance>0 for all pairs, and X/Y room spanning. go build ./... and go test ./internal/simulator/... + full mothership suite + go vet all green.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Implemented DefaultNodePositions(s *Space, count int) []Point in mothership/internal/simulator/node.go — the shared default-geometry source for node seeding. Mirrors cmd/sim's generateNodePositions: 1 node at room center, 2 on a diagonal, 3+ on an expanding ceil(sqrt(count)) grid that always spans two columns and two rows (both X and Y range across the room) with alternating low/high Z for height diversity. Every grid cell is distinct so no two nodes co-locate, keeping the Fresnel excess path |P-T|+|P-R|-|T-R| non-degenerate (bf-4q5w core symptom). Added table-driven tests in node_positions_test.go covering count bounds, in-bounds across offset spaces, pairwise distance > 0, room spanning on X and Y, and the single-node center case. Verified: go build ./... ok, go vet ./... ok, go test ./... all pass. Work committed in 1f3e39a (already pushed).

title: Wire fusion engine node positions at mothership startup
description:
Split from bf-4q5w (parent umbrella). The live 10Hz fusion/blob loop in mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go (~line 1864 onward) produces zero blobs because node positions are never seeded into the blob-producing fusion engine.

Findings:
- Startup DOES seed positions today, but only into selfImprovingLocalizer (the internal/localization 2D engine) at main.go ~1019-1025. It does NOT seed the internal/fusion 3D Fresnel engine (internal/fusion/fusion.go:93 NewEngine; SetNodePosition at :127) that the IO-6 / blob path depends on.
- internal/fusion.NewEngine appears to be constructed only in tests (no non-test construction found). The implementer must first confirm which engine actually feeds the live blob/detection loop and the IO-6 gate, then ensure that engine is both constructed AND seeded.

Scope of THIS child:
- Determine definitively which fusion engine produces the blobs observed by tests/e2e and the IO-6 hard-gate (walker produces a tracked blob).
- Ensure that engine is constructed and integrated into the live loop in cmd/mothership/main.go (construct it if currently test-only).
- At startup, seed every node position into THAT engine from the fleet registry / DB nodes table (PosX/PosY/PosZ), mirroring the existing selfImprovingLocalizer seeding loop, so nodes are NOT all co-located at the DB default of (0,0,1) per internal/db/migrations.go:143-145.

Acceptance criteria:
- The blob-producing fusion engine is constructed in non-test code and integrated into the live loop.
- At startup the engine holds a distinct, non-default position for each node (no all-(0,0,1) collapse).
- A host/unit test or logged assertion confirms engine positions are populated after startup for N nodes.
- go build ./... and existing tests pass.
Note: runtime PATCH/registration propagation and sim geometry are separate children.

Constructed in non-test code (main.go:1028), drives the live 10Hz loop (fusionEngine.Fuse at main.go:1947), publishes peaks via pm.SetTrackedBlobs (main.go:1949). The 2D selfImprovingLocalizer.Fuse runs in a separate slower weight-learning loop (main.go:2676), not the blob path. title: Command palette (Component 34)
description:
## Goal
Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) opens universal search and command interface. Invisible to casual users, indispensable for power users.

## Scope
Search:
- 'kitchen' → Kitchen zone, kitchen nodes, kitchen automations, recent kitchen events
- 'alice' → Alice's current location, today's timeline, sleep report, BLE devices
- 'node 3' → Node details, diagnostics, link health

Navigate time:
- 'last night 2am' → timeline jumps there
- 'yesterday kitchen' → filters timeline to kitchen events yesterday
- 'this morning' → jumps to first detection today

Execute commands:
- 'update all nodes', 're-baseline kitchen', 'add node', 'arm security', 'disarm security'
- 'dark mode'/'light mode', 'export config', 'restart node kitchen-north'

Get help:
- 'help fall detection', 'why false positive', 'troubleshoot kitchen'

Behavior:
- Fuzzy matching: 'flr pln' matches 'Floor Plan settings'
- Recently used commands appear first
- Results show keyboard shortcut hints where applicable
- Escape closes, Enter executes top result
- Works in expert mode only

## Implementation
Frontend-only component
Command registry maps keywords to actions
Search runs against: zone names, person names, node names, setting names, help topics

## Acceptance
- Ctrl+K/Cmd+K opens command palette
- Search finds zones, people, nodes, settings, help topics
- Commands execute correctly
- Time navigation jumps to correct moments
- Fuzzy matching works
- Escape closes palette
close_reason:
Component 34: Command Palette fully implemented and tested.

- Ctrl+K/Cmd+K opens universal search and command interface

- Time navigation: @3am, @-30min, @yesterday 11pm, @this morning, @last night
- Commands execute: arm/disarm security, dark/light mode, export config, re-baseline, add node, etc.
- Recently used commands appear first (up to 5)
- Escape closes, Enter executes top result
- Works in expert mode only (excluded from simple/ambient modes)
- All 88 tests pass

title: Propagate runtime node position changes to the fusion engine
description:
Split from bf-4q5w (parent umbrella). Depends on the startup-seeding child.

Today PATCH /api/nodes/:mac updates only the fleet registry: internal/fleet/handler.go updateNodePosition calls h.mgr.GetRegistry().SetNodePosition (~handler.go:391) and BroadcastRegistry, but never forwards the new position to the fusion engine. Likewise node connect/register events do not push positions to the engine, so a node moved at runtime stays at its stale position in the blob engine.

Scope:
- On PATCH node position (handler.go updateNodePosition), after updating the registry, call the blob-producing fusion engine SetNodePosition(mac,x,y,z) for the engine wired in the prior child.
- On node connect/register, push the node current position to the engine as well.
- Decide where the engine reference lives so the handler can reach it (e.g. via the registry/fleet manager or a setter injected at startup), matching existing accessor patterns such as SetNodePositionAccessor in internal/diagnostics and internal/fleet/weather.go.

Acceptance criteria:
- PATCH /api/nodes/:mac and node registration both update the fusion engine position for that MAC.
- Existing fleet handler tests (internal/fleet/handler_test.go) still pass.
- A test asserts the position the engine stores for a MAC changes after a PATCH with new coordinates.
- go build ./... passes.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Implemented bf-3p6g: runtime node positions now propagate to the fusion engine. Added Manager.nodePositionSink (write-side mirror of the read-side SetNodePositionAccessor used by diagnostics/weather.go), wired to fusionEngine.SetNodePosition in main.go. Fires from both position paths: PATCH /api/nodes/{mac}/position (handler.updateNodePosition -> ForwardNodePosition) and node connect/register (OnNodeConnected forwards the registry position). Commit a17bdd2. Tests: TestHandlerUpdateNodePositionForwardsToEngine (asserts the engine's stored position for a MAC changes after a PATCH with new coords), TestManagerOnNodeConnectedForwardsPositionToEngine (asserts connect seeds the engine position). Existing fleet handler tests still pass; go build/vet/test ./... all green.

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Implemented and verified (commit a17bdd2, pushed to origin/main).

The fleet Manager gains a nodePositionSink — the write-side mirror of the read-side SetNodePositionAccessor used by diagnostics/weather.go — wired at startup (main.go) to fusionEngine.SetNodePosition. It fires from both position paths:
- PATCH /api/nodes/{mac}/position: Handler.updateNodePosition calls ForwardNodePosition after the registry accepts the new position.
- Node connect/register: Manager.OnNodeConnected forwards the node's current registry position.

Tests (internal/fleet/handler_test.go):
- TestHandlerUpdateNodePositionForwardsToEngine: asserts the engine's stored position for a MAC changes after a PATCH with new coordinates (seeds 1.5,2.5,3.5 then updates to 7,8,9).
- TestManagerOnNodeConnectedForwardsPositionToEngine: asserts connect pushes the registry position to the engine.

Verification: go build ./... OK, go vet ./... OK, go test ./... all pass (existing fleet handler tests still green).

title: Capture baseline and verify presence detection with first node
description:
After bf-2po1 (node connected and streaming CSI):

In passive radar mode (single node, using home AP beacons as TX source):
1. Ensure room is empty, then POST /api/baseline/capture to capture empty-room baseline
   curl -X POST https://spaxel.ardenone.com/api/baseline/capture (auth required)
2. Wait 30s for baseline to stabilize (EMA with tau=30s)
3. Walk through the detection area between the node and the home WiFi AP
4. In the dashboard, observe: deltaRMS should spike from ~0.02 to ~0.10+
5. A blob should appear in the 3D visualization at the detected position

With a single node, only presence detection is reliable (not precise localization).
For localization, a second node on the opposite side of the space is needed.

Success: deltaRMS > 0.05 when person is present, blob visible in dashboard.

Troubleshooting: if no signal change, check passive_bss NVS key has the AP BSSID,
or switch node to TX_RX mode and use node-to-node CSI instead of passive radar.

title: Verify dashboard JS/TS identity-field surface
description:
## Objective
Verify the dashboard JS/TS blob surface declares and correctly surfaces the three canonical identity fields (personName, assignedColor, identityResolved), aligning field names with the Go JSON tags emitted by the backend projections. This closes the Tier-4 dashboard track of notes/bf-1q3m-consolidated.md §6 (#7/#8/#9).

## Scope (grounded in notes/bf-1q3m-consolidated.md §3.4 + §6 Tier-4)
- Confirm `dashboard/types/spaxel.d.ts` Blob interface (lines 10-91) declares `personName`, `assignedColor`, `identityResolved` (preliminary grep shows all three present at lines 53/74/90 — verify exact, including the deprecated personLabel/personColor aliases).
- Cross-check the TS field names against the Go JSON tags emitted by the projections that feed the dashboard: `dashboard.blobJSON` (internal/dashboard/hub.go), `api.Track` (internal/api/tracks.go), and — once children 1 & 2 land — `explainability.BlobSnapshot` and `volume.BlobPos`. Document the mapping; adjust TS or Go tags only if a real mismatch is found.
- Confirm `dashboard/js/state.js:290` creation literal (`appState.blobs[id] = { id: id }`) correctly surfaces server-supplied identity via the `Object.assign(...)` at state.js:292 — no pre-init needed if the server sends the fields (it does, post-Tier-1).
- `dashboard/js/websocket.js:167` (J2 dead-reckoning cache) is intentionally identity-free — confirm and leave alone.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] spaxel.d.ts Blob interface confirmed to declare personName, assignedColor, identityResolved (+ deprecated aliases)
- [ ] TS field names ↔ Go JSON-tag mapping documented; any mismatch fixed
- [ ] state.js:290 creation path confirmed to surface server identity via Object.assign; no change needed unless broken

Cross-checked TS vs Go JSON tags across all 8 blob-shaped types: 7 already camelCase; FIXED volume.BlobPos — the sole outlier emitting snake_case (person_name/assigned_color/identity_resolved -> camelCase) to match the canonical convention (bf-5151) and the dashboard. Tags were inert (volume.BlobPos is consumed in Go by Store.Evaluate, which returns fired-trigger IDs; never marshalled to the dashboard) but would have leaked snake_case if ever surfaced. state.js:290 surfaces server identity via Object.assign (no change needed); websocket.js:167 dead-reckoning cache intentionally identity-free (left alone). go vet ./... and go test ./... pass. title: Verify position propagation through registry and fusion engine
description:

- Verify positions reach the fusion engine accumulation grid
- Add any missing glue if positions don't flow through (should be wired from prior children)

Acceptance criteria:

- Positions from registry reach the fusion engine correctly
- If wiring was incomplete, add the missing connections
- Integration test or log output shows positions flowing through all stages

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Closed by verification. Position propagation through the full chain (simulator VirtualNodeStore -> registry_bridge -> fleet.Registry SQLite -> fusion.Engine.nodePos mirror -> accumulation grid) was already wired by prior children (bf-5lii -> bf-69ym -> bf-u7ds -> bf-95tx).

This bead's acceptance criterion -- 'positions reach the fusion engine accumulation grid and actually drive localization' -- is locked in by TestPositionPropagation_ReachesAccumulationGrid in mothership/internal/api/simulator_test.go. It posts two explicitly-positioned nodes via the simulator REST API, runs one Fuse step with a link between the two MACs the engine now holds, and asserts a blob localizes near the link midpoint (confidence 1.0, 1 active link). That peak is only reachable if BOTH endpoints' positions reached the grid lookup (AddLinkInfluence reads nodePos[mac] for both endpoints; a missing/co-located position yields no peak).

Verified: go test ./... PASS (internal/api incl.), go vet ./... clean. Grid trace under -v:
  [grid] position propagation  midpoint=(3.00,1.00)  peak=(2.90,0.90,0.90) conf=1.000 activeLinks=1

No production wiring change needed -- prior children completed it. Committed and pushed to origin/main.

The wiring from prior children is complete and correct: a posted node position flows VirtualNodeStore → fleetRegistryBridge.SyncToRegistry (SQLite write) → fleetRegistryAdapter forwardPos hook → fleetMgr.ForwardNodePosition → nodePositionSink → fusion.Engine.SetNodePosition, and Fuse reads nodePos[mac] for both endpoints into AddLinkInfluence on the accumulation grid. No production change was needed.
